articles.description: The federal government has selected eight proposals to test electric aircraft across 26 states.

articles.title: OpenAI and Google employees rush to Anthropic’s defense in DOD lawsuit
articles.description: More than 30 OpenAI and Google DeepMind employees signed onto a statement supporting Anthropic's lawsuit against the Defense Department after the agency labeled the AI firm a supply-chain risk, according to court filings.

articles.title: Anthropic launches code review tool to check flood of AI-generated code
articles.description: Anthropic launched Code Review in Claude Code, a multi-agent system that automatically analyzes AI-generated code, flags logic errors, and helps enterprise developers manage the growing volume of code produced with AI.

articles.title: Bluesky CEO Jay Graber steps down
articles.description: Graber explained that, as a more mature company, Bluesky needs a "seasoned operator focused on scaling and execution." Graber said she feels better suited to building Bluesky's technology itself.

articles.title: It looks like the DOJ isn’t going to break up Live Nation and Ticketmaster
articles.description: The DOJ and Live Nation agreed to a tentative settlement, but dozens of state attorneys general don't want to drop the lawsuit.

articles.title: Periwinkle is making self-hosted social media on Bluesky’s AT Protocol even easier
articles.description: Periwinkle offers managed hosting for AT Protocol users who want social media accounts on their own domains, with backups, storage, and migration tools.

articles.title: OpenAI acquires Promptfoo to secure its AI agents
articles.description: This deal underscores how frontier labs are scrambling to prove their technology can be used safely in critical business operations.

articles.title: GM figured out how to navigate EV uncertainty with the Chevy Bolt
articles.description: The 2027 Chevy Bolt might not be a groundbreaking new EV, but incremental improvements have made it better without inflating the price.

articles.title: Anthropic sues Defense Department over supply-chain risk designation
articles.description: Anthropic filed suit against the Department of Defense on Monday after the agency labeled it a supply-chain risk. The complaint calls the DOD's actions "unprecedented and unlawful."

articles.title: Slate Auto changes CEO months ahead of affordable EV launch
articles.description: Former Amazon Marketplace vice president Peter Faricy is the new person in charge of the company.

articles.title: Xprize founder Peter Diamandis launches new contest to manifest a new ‘Star Trek’
articles.description: The famed author and investor has launched the Future Vision Xprize with sponsors like Google, Marc Benioff, and Ben Horowitz to fund optimistic sci-fi films.

articles.title: Qualcomm’s partnership with Neura Robotics is just the beginning
articles.description: Neura Robotics is going to build new robots on top of Qualcomm's new IQ10 processors that were released at CES.

articles.title: Russian government hackers targeting Signal and WhatsApp users, Dutch spies warn
articles.description: Dutch intelligence is accusing Russia-backed hackers of running a “large-scale global” hacking campaign against Signal and WhatsApp users.

digest:
## 🔬 Major Technical Developments

### JSLinux Now Supports x86_64
URL: https://bellard.org/jslinux/
Fabrice Bellard's browser-based Linux emulator adds 64-bit support — a technical flex that signals WebAssembly's maturing capabilities.
Bellard is one of computing's quiet legends (FFmpeg, QEMU), and this update demonstrates that browser-based virtualization is approaching practical utility. For enterprise IT, this opens doors to secure, sandboxed environments that require zero local installation — relevant for training, demos, and air-gapped access scenarios.

### Anthropic launches code review tool to check flood of AI-generated code
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/09/anthropic-launches-code-review-tool-to-check-flood-of-ai-generated-code/
Multi-agent system automates quality control for AI-generated code — Anthropic is building the picks and shovels for the vibe-coding gold rush.
This is a defensive moat play. As AI coding tools proliferate, the bottleneck shifts from generation to verification. Anthropic is positioning Claude Code as an end-to-end development platform, not just a code generator. Enterprise adoption depends on trust, and automated review addresses the "who checks the AI's work" problem that's blocking procurement.

### DARPA's new X-76
URL: https://www.darpa.mil/news/2026/darpa-new-x-76-speed-of-jet-freedom-of-helicopter
New experimental aircraft combines jet speed with helicopter VTOL flexibility — defense industrial base gets a next-gen platform.
Watch the contractor announcements. Programs like this typically signal 5-10 year procurement cycles worth billions. The VTOL-plus-speed combination suggests urban deployment scenarios and carrier operations without runways — relevant for Pacific theater planning.

## 💼 Business & Market Signals

### OpenAI is walking away from expanding its Stargate data center with Oracle
URL: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/09/oracle-is-building-yesterdays-data-centers-with-tomorrows-debt.html
OpenAI-Oracle partnership fraying — infrastructure deals in AI are proving harder to execute than announce.
The Stargate announcement was a $500B headline. Walking away signals either capital constraints at OpenAI, execution problems at Oracle, or both. Oracle's cloud business needed this win; the stock is vulnerable. More broadly, this suggests the hyperscale AI infrastructure buildout is hitting real-world friction — permitting, power, and perhaps demand uncertainty.

### Anthropic sues Defense Department over supply-chain risk designation
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/09/anthropic-sues-defense-department-over-supply-chain-risk-designation/
Anthropic fights DOD blacklist in federal court — the AI industry's relationship with national security apparatus is fracturing along company lines.
This is significant. DOD supply-chain designations typically target foreign adversaries, not San Francisco startups. The designation likely relates to Anthropic's safety-focused positioning and potential reluctance on certain military applications. OpenAI and Google employees signing in support (per related filing) suggests unusual cross-company solidarity — the AI labs may be circling wagons against government overreach.

### OpenAI acquires Promptfoo to secure its AI agents
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/09/openai-acquires-promptfoo-to-secure-its-ai-agents/
OpenAI buys prompt security tooling — agent safety is now an M&A category.
Promptfoo was the leading open-source tool for testing prompt injection and jailbreaks. OpenAI acquiring it signals agents are close to production deployment and security is the gating factor. Expect more acqui-hires in AI security; the talent pool is tiny and the stakes are rising.

### Bluesky CEO Jay Graber steps down
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/09/bluesky-ceo-jay-graber-steps-down/
Founder-to-operator transition at Bluesky — the company is growing up, for better or worse.
Graber moving to a technical role while bringing in a "seasoned operator" is textbook Series B playbook. Bluesky has momentum post-Twitter exodus but needs to monetize. The question is whether professional management dilutes the decentralization ethos that attracted users in the first place.

## 📋 Case Studies & Deep Dives

### No, it doesn't cost Anthropic $5k per Claude Code user
URL: https://martinalderson.com/posts/no-it-doesnt-cost-anthropic-5k-per-claude-code-user/
Unit economics teardown challenges viral cost estimates — the math on AI subscription pricing is more nuanced than Twitter takes suggest.
This is useful for anyone modeling AI company financials. The viral $5K/user figure likely conflated compute costs with infrastructure amortization and ignored utilization patterns. Real unit economics depend heavily on user behavior distributions — power users subsidized by casual ones. Worth reading before your next AI investment memo.

### Is legal the same as legitimate: AI reimplementation and the erosion of copyleft
URL: https://writings.hongminhee.org/2026/03/legal-vs-legitimate/
Legal analysis of AI model training on copyleft code — the GPL's future is genuinely uncertain.
This matters for software supply chain risk. If courts determine that AI training constitutes a derivative work, major models may face retroactive licensing obligations. If they don't, copyleft as a licensing strategy may be functionally dead. Either outcome reshapes open source economics.

### An opinionated take on how to do important research that matters
URL: https://nicholas.carlini.com/writing/2026/how-to-win-a-best-paper-award.html
Nicholas Carlini on research strategy — a window into how top AI researchers think about impact.
Carlini is a leading ML security researcher. His framework — focus on problems that will matter in 3 years, not problems that matter today — is relevant beyond academia. For investors, understanding researcher incentives helps predict where breakthrough papers will emerge.

## ⚡ Signal vs. Noise

The Anthropic-DOD lawsuit is the week's real story. We're watching the AI industry's relationship with government fragment in real-time: OpenAI chasing defense contracts, Anthropic getting blacklisted, employees crossing company lines to protest. Infrastructure deals are harder to close than announce (see: Stargate), while the tooling layer around AI coding — security, review, testing — is becoming the actual value capture point. The picks-and-shovels thesis is playing out faster than the model-layer competition.
